topic.what: Integration of formal verification and process mining for scalable security verification of automotive network architectures using Attack Resilience Hyperproperties
topic.how: Proposes CRASH adversarial model and ARH verification orchestration algorithm combined with process mining of hypertraces to identify attacker behavior causing security property invalidations
topic.why_matters: Addresses state-space explosion in formal verification enabling comprehensive analysis of compromise impact on security properties in automotive networks under expected component compromise

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of compromise-capability-SSP combinations requiring verification
characteristics.evidence: Section 3.3 case study with 4 entities, 3 domains, 2 permissions, 2 SPs yields 4^7 * 2 scenarios
characteristics.context: Exhaustive verification would require 9+ hours at 1s per scenario
Under optimistic assumption of 1s per verification lemma

characteristics.evidence: Section 3.3 complexity analysis shows |P|^|E| growth; preorder traversal with monotonicity pruning reduces verification count
characteristics.context: Complexity grows exponentially in number of entities and linearly in number of security properties

characteristics.what_it_measures: Process mining model captures only invalidating attacker behavior without generalization
characteristics.evidence: Section 5.4 states no generalization; model captures only trace-contained activities for invalidating traces
characteristics.context: Achieved through exploratory PM approach focused on attacker behavior patterns rather than complete process flows

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of entities and domains in case study ANA
characteristics.evidence: Section 5.3 evaluation describes UseCase, DGW, TCU, Target entities with Inner, InternetFacing, Backend domains
characteristics.context: Industrial relevance demonstrated through automotive network abstraction

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of security properties evaluated in case study
characteristics.evidence: Section 2.1 defines secrecy and authenticity properties for BMS protocol
characteristics.context: Comparative analysis enables identification of component-permission combinations across SPs
